pattern: Split the queue into two halves and keep their sizes within one of each other; the boundary between the halves is always the middle, so every front/middle/back push or pop only ever touches the two ends of the two halves plus one O(1) rebalance step.
hints: A single array works but shifting elements for a middle insert/removal is O(n); think about keeping two separate double-ended sequences, a front half and a back half, so every operation only touches an end of one of them.

Maintain the invariant that the front half's size is always equal to the back half's size, or exactly one larger. Whichever end an operation targets, apply it to the matching half's front or back, then restore the invariant by moving one element between the halves if it slipped.

The 'frontmost' middle element is always the LAST element of the front half (never the back half's first) once you keep the front half sized >= the back half: pushMiddle appends to the front half (after shifting its overflow into the back half first if the halves were already equal), and popMiddle always removes the front half's last element.

edge_inputs.description: statement example: push to all three positions then pop from all three until empty
single element: every pop variant sees the same lone value, and the queue is empty afterward
all pops on a brand-new empty queue return -1
pushMiddle into an even-length queue picks the frontmost of the two middle slots (goes just before the true center)
popMiddle on an even-length queue returns the frontmost of the two middle candidates
build a queue purely from pushFront calls (reverse order), then drain purely from the back

stress.description: long randomized mix of all six operations against a shared queue
stress.note: roughly even mix of pushes and pops so the queue frequently empties and refills, exercising the empty-queue -1 path often
